qa_pairs.question: What is the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) is an annual process that includes two main reviews: a peer-review by over 90 discipline-specific experts from Canadian academic institutions who assess and rate computational research projects, and a technical review by Federation staff who verify the accuracy of requested resources and recommend national system allocations. The Resource Access Program Administrative Committee oversees the entire process.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to submit an application for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: To be eligible to submit a RAC application, Principal Investigators (PIs) and Co-PIs must be faculty members at a Canadian academic institution and possess an active Alliance account with an Academic Principal Investigator role, such as Faculty, Adjunct Faculty, or Librarian. Applicants are advised to ensure their primary, most up-to-date position is reflected in their CCDB account before applying.

qa_pairs.question: What types of advanced computing resources are available for researchers to access through the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Through the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC), researchers can request access to a broad spectrum of advanced computing services. These include general-purpose systems, large shared memory systems, general-purpose computing on graphics processing units (GPUs), storage, and cloud resources. Technical support and training are also provided.
